title: Fear Garden — Level 1 Training Detective
description: LEVEL 1 TRAINING DETECTIVE CASE version of Fear Garden with unique suspect, method, motive, evidence, and answers.

answerKey.method: Loosened the hanging planter rig above the courtyard stage
answerKey.motive: To stop Carter from exposing her stolen charity raffle money
answerKey.keyEvidence: Planter rig photo, raffle cash spreadsheet, and maya’s staff-key log
answerKey.explanation: Maya Bell had the access, timing, and motive. The proof path combines planter rig photo, raffle cash spreadsheet, and Maya’s staff-key log with the failed statement: Maya said she never entered storage.

publicClues.title: Case Opened
publicClues.text: Fear Garden begins at Beer Garden in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. This session is Level 1: Training Detective Case.

publicClues.title: Difficulty Loaded
publicClues.text: This is Level 1: Training Detective Case. Evidence and answers are unique to this level.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 1 Available
publicClues.text: Round 1 is ready. Answer the checkpoint after reviewing the body scene.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 2 Available
publicClues.text: Round 2 is ready. Compare the timeline before answering.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 3 Available
publicClues.text: Round 3 is ready. Digital and venue systems now matter.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 4 Available
publicClues.text: Round 4 is ready. Find the motive evidence.

publicClues.title: Final Accusation Open
publicClues.text: Final questions are open. Complete all 10 total questions before the lock.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Carter Reed missed two calls from Maya Bell before the garden music cut out.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: A final call lasted eleven seconds as Carter moved toward the side corridor.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Carter texted Maya: “We talk tonight. I saw the numbers.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: Maya replied: “Not at the bar. Meet by the garden gate.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Carter’s phone pinged near the patio garden path at 8:07 PM.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Maya’s device appears nearby during the same short window.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A Beer Garden event ledger shows a suspicious private payout connected to stop carter from exposing her stolen charity raffle money.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: The payout trail links to a device or account controlled by Maya.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A guest photo shows Maya near the restricted service path before the incident.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: Another photo captures the scene detail: loose planter rig over Carter’s table.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: A patio livestream catches a missing gap just before Carter disappears from frame.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: The posted version removes the seconds that would show who crossed behind the garden wall.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: Maya saved a contact under “Garden Services,” matching a suspicious payment line.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: Carter saved the same number with the note “ask about fake paperwork.”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: the killer needed venue access, timing, and a reason to silence Carter.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the scene points to planning, not a random Beer Garden accident.

files.title: Files Evidence 1
files.text: A security export skips the exact Beer Garden patio window.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: File metadata connects the edit to the suspect’s access trail: Maya Bell.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 1
browser.text: A browser search asks how to remove timestamp metadata from venue clips.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2
browser.text: Another search matches the method: loosened the hanging planter rig above the courtyard stage.

rounds.title: Round 1: The Body in the Garden
rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the body in the garden and answer the checkpoint question.
rounds.dialogue: Round 1 is open. Focus on the body in the garden. The clues in this round test one part of the Fear Garden case logic.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 1 is nearly complete. Lock in what the body in the garden proves before the next round opens.

rounds.dialogue: Round 4 is open. Focus on the money root. The clues in this round test one part of the Fear Garden case logic.

rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the cover-up and answer the checkpoint question.

rounds.title: Final Accusation
rounds.objective: Answer the final five questions to close Fear Garden.
rounds.dialogue: The final accusation window is open. Choose the culprit, method, motive, key evidence, and the statement that fails.
rounds.countdownReview: The final window is closing. Submit all answers before Fear Garden locks.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Fear Garden at Beer Garden in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. You are playing Level 1: Training Detective Case. Study each app carefully. Every level has its own truth path, evidence trail, and final answer set.
revealScript: Case closed. This was Level 1: Training Detective Case. The culprit was Maya Bell. The method: Loosened the hanging planter rig above the courtyard stage. The motive: To stop Carter from exposing her stolen charity raffle money. The proof: Planter rig photo, raffle cash spreadsheet, and maya’s staff-key log.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: What makes Carter’s death suspicious?

options.label: A normal spilled pint near the stage
loose planter rig over Carter’s table
A table number written backward
A guest closing a patio umbrella

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 2: Which timeline clue matters most?

options.label: The DJ restarted a playlist
A guest paid with cash
Maya’s key opened the storage closet before the fall
A server dropped a tray outside

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 3: Which digital clue suggests planning?

options.label: A low battery warning
A misspelled hashtag
A normal Wi-Fi reconnect
a raffle cash spreadsheet missing two deposits

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 4: What clue points toward motive?

options.label: Maya’s apron fiber caught on the planter hook
A birthday receipt
A lost wristband
An ordinary tab transfer

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 5: Which statement begins to collapse?

options.label: Maya said the plants were watered
Maya said she never entered storage
Janelle said the gate was busy
Darius said the speaker was loud
Trevor Knox
Janelle Price
Maya Bell
Darius Cole

questions.prompt: Final 2: How was the murder carried out?

options.label: Poisoned a shared punch bowl
Cut the stage lights only
Staged a random parking-lot fall
Loosened the hanging planter rig above the courtyard stage
To stop Carter from exposing her stolen charity raffle money
To win a trivia prize
To protect a karaoke score
To hide a family recipe

questions.prompt: Final 4: Which evidence best proves it?

options.label: A guest rumor and a napkin
Planter rig photo, raffle cash spreadsheet, and maya’s staff-key log
A broken patio chair only
A single missed call with no context

questions.prompt: Final 5: Which suspect statement falls apart?

options.label: Maya said the music was loud
Trevor said the patio was crowded
Maya said she never entered storage
Janelle said the menu was changed
